hero_subtitle: SFTY connects multiple compliance providers into one operational workflow to preserve audit evidence and support smooth provider switching without rebuilding your compliance stack.
trust_card_1_value: Identity checks connected to onboarding decisions
trust_card_2_value: Corporate verification, UBO review and entity risk
trust_card_3_value: Wallet and transaction monitoring in one workflow
trust_card_4_value: Message routing, completeness checks and exception handling
trust_card_5_value: Regulatory changes mapped to internal rule
trust_card_6_value: Decision logs and evidence for every review
problem_description: Compliance teams face operational friction when integrating new providers disrupts established workflows and fragments data connections. This increases manual effort, weakens traceability and slows review times without a unified compliance operating layer.
problem_point_1_value: KYC, KYB, KYT and Travel Rule outputs sit in separate tools
problem_point_2_value: Teams move data between dashboards, spreadsheets and internal notes
problem_point_3_value: Regulatory changes are hard to map into internal workflows
problem_point_4_value: Evidence and decision history are difficult to reconstruct later
solution_description: SFTY integrates outputs from multiple providers, normalizes data, routes cases and preserves decision evidence to maintain consistent compliance operations during provider transitions without disrupting existing workflows.
workflow_title: From provider outputs to compliance decisions
workflow_description: SFTY collects and normalizes multiple provider checks into rule-based reviews that open cases, record decisions, preserve evidence and prepare compliance reports.
workflow_step_1_description: KYC, KYB, KYT and screening outputs enter SFTY
workflow_step_2_description: Risk rules route checks into the right workflow
workflow_step_3_description: A case opens with customer, wallet and provider context
workflow_step_4_description: Teams review, decide and record the outcome
workflow_step_5_description: Logs, decisions and reports stay ready for audit
why_sfty_title: Beyond provider dependency. Structured compliance control
why_sfty_description: SFTY provides a provider-neutral layer that connects diverse outputs into consistent workflows, decisions, evidence and reporting without disrupting existing compliance systems.
why_sfty_card_1_description: Work above existing KYC, KYB, KYT and Travel Rule tools
why_sfty_card_2_description: Turn disconnected outputs into structured reviews and cases
why_sfty_card_3_description: Preserve evidence, reviewer actions and decision history
why_sfty_card_4_description: Give compliance, risk and leadership one operating view
authority_description: SFTY supports VASPs with a provider-neutral compliance layer that preserves audit evidence, enables bank due diligence and supports enterprise-ready deployment control.
authority_card_1_description: Connect KYC, KYB, KYT, Travel Rule and screening providers into one workflow
authority_card_2_description: Preserve provider outputs, reviewer notes, decisions and supporting records
authority_card_3_description: Prepare clearer compliance evidence for banks, partners and external review
authority_card_4_description: Support private environments, permissions, integrations and internal governance
ask_sfty_placeholder: How can SFTY support provider switching without disrupting workflows
ask_sfty_chip_1_message: Explain how SFTY connects multi-provider compliance outputs
ask_sfty_chip_2_message: Show how audit evidence is preserved during provider changes
ask_sfty_chip_3_message: Describe how switching providers affects case and review workflows
ask_sfty_helper_text: AI assistant for product, deployment and compliance questions
faq_description: Clear answers on provider orchestration, Travel Rule workflows, audit evidence and enterprise compliance operations.
faq_1_question: What is SFTY for VASP operations?
faq_1_answer: SFTY serves VASPs by connecting fragmented compliance data into one workflow connecting KYC, KYB, KYT, Travel Rule and screening outputs for better decision-making and oversight.
faq_2_question: How does SFTY handle multi-provider outputs?
faq_2_answer: SFTY normalizes and connects outputs from multiple compliance providers, routes reviews based on rules, opens cases, records decisions and applies regulatory updates in a connected operational layer.
faq_3_question: How does SFTY preserve audit-ready evidence?
faq_3_answer: SFTY records decisions, case history, provider outputs and rule changes with evidence packages to support audit readiness and clear compliance review trails across workflows.
faq_4_question: Can SFTY integrate with existing compliance stacks?
faq_4_answer: Yes. SFTY overlays existing compliance providers and systems, connecting them through configurable integrations without requiring a full rebuild of your current compliance stack.
faq_5_question: Does SFTY replace current compliance providers?
faq_5_answer: No. SFTY acts as a provider-neutral orchestration platform that enhances and connects existing provider outputs rather than replacing underlying compliance tools.
final_title: Bring provider outputs into controlled compliance workflows
final_description: Review how SFTY connects multi-provider results, preserves evidence and supports smooth switching without rebuilding your compliance model.
footer_description: Supervisory and compliance control layer for digital asset markets, businesses and regulated institutions.
